title: toxicodendron undulatum
intro: Toxicodendron undulatum, also known as poison oak, is a plant species that belongs to the Anacardiaceae family, characterized by its leaves with lobed or deeply toothed margins and a distinctive, unpleasant odor. The plant contains urushiol, an oily resin that causes severe skin irritation and allergic reactions in many people. Medicinally, the plant's roots and bark have been traditionally used to treat various ailments, including fever, rheumatism, and skin conditions, due to their anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties. Herbal preparations of Toxicodendron undulatum include teas, tinctures, and salves, which are used to treat skin conditions, reduce inflammation, and promote wound healing.
